name: Halona Blowhole Lookout
tagline: A natural lava tube that shoots ocean spray 30 feet in the air — quick roadside stop with views of the rugged east coast.
parking: Free pullout lot on Kalanianaole Hwy. Small and fills fast midday
bestTime: Best spray during high surf and high tide — winter swells produce the most dramatic blasts. Morning light is best for photos
talkStory: A quick but satisfying stop on the east side drive between Hanauma Bay and Makapu'u. The blowhole shoots water through a lava tube formed by volcanic activity — bigger swells mean bigger blasts, sometimes 30+ feet. The lookout also overlooks Halona Cove (aka 'From Here to Eternity Beach' from the 1953 film). DO NOT climb down the rocks to the blowhole — people have been killed by rogue waves and getting sucked into the tube. Stay behind the guardrail. Combine with Sandy Beach (next door), Hanauma Bay, and Makapu'u Lighthouse Trail for a full east side day.
